content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

February 5, 1991

Robert E. Leidigh  
Olson, Connelly, Hagel, Fong & Leidigh  
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. I-90-554

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice concerning the contribution limits of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). Since your request is general in nature and is not requested on behalf of any specific candidate, we are treating it as a request for informal assistance.

This will confirm prior telephone advice to you that the limits on contributions to candidates contained in the Act do not

---

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

3 The provisions of the Act limiting campaign contributions are generally set forth in Article 3, Sections 85300-85307. On September 25, 1990, The United States District Court, Eastern District of California, in the case of Service Employees International Union, et al. v. Fair Political Practices Commission, Case No. CIVS 89-0433 LKK-JFM (SEIU) ruled that the fiscal year contribution limitations of the Act were unconstitutional. This ruling does not invalidate contribution limitations which are not based upon the fiscal year, such as the special election limitations set forth in Section 85305. The SEIU decision is being appealed.

apply to contributions of the candidate’s community property funds to his or her campaign. We consider the candidate’s community property4 as his or her "personal funds." The Act provides that limitations on contributions by persons to candidates "shall not apply to a candidate’s contribution of his or her personal funds to his or her own campaign contribution account." (Section 85301(b).) However, we conservatively advise that the candidate’s "personal funds" do not include the separate property funds of the candidate’s spouse.

If you have any questions concerning this advice, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Margaret Ellison
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:ME:dg

4 We have not made a distinction between the candidates "share" of the community property and his or her spouse’s "share" of community property. All community property funds of the candidate and his or her spouse are "personal funds" of the candidate for purposes of Section 85301.
